reviews.review_description: The game doesn't seem to recognize the quest pro controllers.  I'm using a quest 3 and stuck at the start of the game with no movement

9 DROPS starts off feeling like a Gorilla Tag-style locomotion game—but it quickly reveals why that movement style is essential: you're going to fall. A lot.

The first level alone has five stages, and I must have died over 200 times, yet I couldn’t stop playing. There’s something oddly addictive about the constant retrying, slowly progressing further each time.

The game’s real strength is in its variety—of both stages and mechanics—which keeps it fresh and replayable.

Hopefully it gains enough traction to introduce multiplayer someday. Racing others while trying not to drop 99 times? Yes, please.

Very fun game—definitely worth a try!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the fantastic review!
We’re so glad you embraced the chaos and kept climbing back after every drop! 
Your feedback on the movement, difficulty, and stage variety means a lot. 
Multiplayer is definitely a fun idea! We’ll keep that in mind as we look ahead!

reviews.review_description:
Interesting concept, but it's a little glitchy for me.
It's kind of jumpy at times and I'm really close to the ground and some parts IN the ground like sitting down on a stand-up game.
Also, there's no option menu to do any kind of adjustments..

developer_response.body:
Thanks for sharing your feedback. We checked with our dev team and found that clipping into the ground can happen more often on Quest 2. If you could let us know more details, it would be a big help—you can either report it through our inquiry form <https://forms.gle/QsxV2h1CENQxwECf9> or on our Discord <https://discord.com/invite/univrs>.

We’ve also shared your feedback about an adjustment menu with the team for future consideration.

reviews.review_description: I have played it and beat it, overall very cool. I liked that it had some sort of gorilla tag movement with tweaked physics to purposely mess up the jumping for extra difficulty. the idea of it alone is good enough too. overall 5 stars! would recommend.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the great review.
We’re glad you enjoyed the challenge and unique movement style, and we appreciate your recommendation for 99 DROPS!

reviews.review_description: I realy enjoyed the Frustration...challanging and fair... First GT Style Game i Like... Fair price...☺️👍

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for the kind words!
We’re thrilled to hear you enjoyed the challenge and found the game fair and fun.
It means a lot to us that this is the first GT-style game you liked — we’ll keep working hard to make it even better!

reviews.review_description: 99 Drops is easily one of the best Gorilla Tag-style games out there. The movement mechanics feel super smooth and responsive, and the design keeps things fresh and exciting, the gameplay is intense and addicting in the best way and the visuals are clean. If you’re into fast-paced VR movement games, 99 Drops is a must-play. Can’t wait to see where it goes from here!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the awesome review!
We're thrilled to hear you're enjoying the fast-paced movement and style of 99 DROPS. More fun updates are definitely on the way, so stay tuned!
